chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with severe anemia-induced type 2 myocardial infarction.
history: The patient had significant prior atherosclerotic coronary disease and experienced acute bleeding, which led to severe anemia. He had extensive ST depression on ECG.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm. Physical examination revealed no specific findings.
labs: ECG: extensive ST depression. Blood tests: hemoglobin <80 g/L, high sensitivity troponin T and creatine kinase levels slightly elevated.
